content:
# John Chapter 13 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_013_page_001.jpg, JHN_013_page_002.jpg

1. Now before the feast of the Passover, the Yeshua knowing that the hour of him had come that he should depart from this world to the Father, having loved the own who [were] in the world, to [the] end he loved them.

2. And supper happening, the devil already having put into the heart of Ioudas [son] of Simon Iskariotes that he should betray him,

3. knowing that the Father has given all things into the hands of him, and that from God he came forth and to the God he goes,

4. he rises from the supper and lays down the garments, and having taken a towel, he girded himself.

5. Then he puts water into the basin and began to wash the feet of the disciples and to wipe [them] with the towel with which he was girded.

6. Then he comes to Simon Petros. He says to him, "Lord, you wash the feet of me?"

7. The Yeshua answered and said to him, "What I do you do not know now, but you will know after these things."

8. Petros says to him, "You will never wash the feet of me." The Yeshua answered him, "If I do not wash you, you have no part with me."

9. Simon Petros says to him, "Lord, not the feet of me only, but also the hands and the head."

10. The Yeshua says to him, "The [one] having been bathed has no need except the feet to wash, but is clean wholly; and you are clean, but not all."

11. For he knew the [one] betraying him; because of this he said, "Not all of you are clean."

12. When therefore he had washed the feet of them and taken the garments of him and reclined again, he said to them, "Do you know what I have done to you?

13. You call me 'the Teacher' and 'the Lord,' and well you say, for I am.

14. If therefore I, the Lord and the Teacher, washed the feet of you, you also ought to wash one another's feet.

15. For an example I gave you, that just as I did to you, you also should do.

16. Amen, amen, I say to you, a slave is not greater than the lord of him, nor [is] one sent greater than the [one] having sent him.

17. If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them.

18. I do not speak concerning all of you — I know whom I chose — but that the scripture may be fulfilled, 'The [one] eating the bread of me lifted up against me the heel of him.'

19. From now I tell you before it happens, that when it happens you may believe that I am.

20. Amen, amen, I say to you, the [one] receiving whomever I send receives me, and the [one] receiving me receives the [one] having sent me."

21. Having said these things, the Yeshua was troubled in the spirit and testified and said, "Amen, amen, I say to you that one of you will betray me."

22. The disciples looked at one another, at a loss [as to] whom he speaks about.

23. One of the disciples of him was reclining at the bosom of the Yeshua, whom the Yeshua loved.

24. Then Simon Petros nods to this [one] and says to him, "Say who it is concerning whom he speaks."

25. Then that [one], having leaned back thus upon the breast of the Yeshua, says to him, "Lord, who is it?"

26. Then the Yeshua answers, "It is that [one] for whom I will dip the morsel and give [it] to him." Then having dipped the morsel, he takes [it] and gives [it] to Ioudas [son] of Simon Iskariotes.

27. And after the morsel, then the Satan entered into that [one]. Then the Yeshua says to him, "What you do, do quickly."

28. But none of the [ones] reclining knew why he said this to him.

29. For some thought, since Ioudas had the money box, that the Yeshua says to him, "Buy what things we have need of for the feast," or that he should give something to the poor.

30. Then having received the morsel, that [one] went out immediately. And it was night.

31. When therefore he went out, the Yeshua says, "Now the Son of the Man has been glorified, and the God has been glorified in him.

32. If the God has been glorified in him, the God also will glorify him in himself, and immediately he will glorify him.

33. Little children, still a little [while] I am with you. You will seek me, and just as I said to the Ioudaioi, 'Where I go, you are not able to come,' so now I say to you.

34. A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another; just as I loved you, that you also love one another.

35. By this all will know that you are disciples of me, if you have love among one another."

36. Simon Petros says to him, "Lord, where are you going?" The Yeshua answered, "Where I go, you are not able to follow me now, but you will follow later."

37. Petros says to him, "Lord, why am I not able to follow you now? The life of me I will lay down for you."

38. The Yeshua answers, "The life of you you will lay down for me? Amen, amen, I say to you, [the] rooster will by no means crow until you deny me three times."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "To the end he loved them":** εἰς τέλος ἠγάπησεν αὐτούς — "to the end" (εἰς τέλος) can mean "to the uttermost" (in quality/degree) or "to the end" (in time). Both meanings apply: Jesus loved them completely and to the very end. This verse is the theological heading for the entire Farewell Discourse.

2. **v.2 — Textual note:** Sinaiticus reads "supper happening" (δείπνου γινομένου) — present tense, "during supper" — not "supper being ended" (γενομένου), as in some manuscripts.

3. **v.10 — "The one having been bathed":** ὁ λελουμένος — the perfect participle indicates a completed bath. Jesus distinguishes between the complete washing (λούω, the full bath) and the partial washing (νίπτω, washing feet). The disciples are already "clean" (through faith/word); only their feet (daily contamination) need washing.

4. **v.18 — Psalm 41:9 citation:** "The one eating my bread has lifted up his heel against me." The Hebrew of Psalm 41 reads "my close friend" (literally "man of my peace"). The betrayal is intimate — Judas shares table fellowship with Jesus before betraying him.

5. **v.23 — "The disciple whom Jesus loved":** The Beloved Disciple's first appearance by this designation. He reclines "at the bosom" (ἐν τῷ κόλπῳ) of Jesus — the same phrase used of the Son's relationship to the Father in 1:18. The Beloved Disciple mirrors the Son's intimacy with the Father.

6. **v.30 — "And it was night":** καὶ ἦν νύξ — the most theologically freighted three words in John. Judas goes out from the light (Jesus) into the darkness. This is not merely a time reference but a cosmic statement: the betrayer enters the realm of darkness.

— EMET, AI Translator
